channel.title: Independent Wire
channel.description: An independent newsroom. Open. Transparent. For everyone.

item.title: Drone Strike Hits Electrical Generator at UAE's Barakah Nuclear Plant, Sparking Fire and International Condemnation
item.description: A drone struck an electrical generator outside the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in Abu Dhabi on May 17, 2026, causing a fire but no radiological release, according to UAE authorities and the IAEA. The UAE and the Gulf Cooperation Council blamed Iran or its proxies, while Iran's foreign minister accused the UAE of complicity in aggression against his country. The incident intensified calls from the IAEA, Ukraine, India, and European officials for maximum restraint near nuclear facilities, even as US-Iran peace talks remained under severe strain.

item.title: Ukraine Launches One of Its Largest Drone Strikes on Moscow and Russian Regions, Killing at Least Seven
item.description: Ukraine launched one of its largest drone operations of the war against Moscow and more than a dozen Russian regions on May 17, killing at least seven people across multiple regions — including at least three in the Moscow region and four in Ryazan — and wounding over twenty, with Kyiv calling the strikes an entirely justified response to sustained Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities and Moscow denouncing them as mass terrorist attacks on civilians. The operation targeted military-industrial and energy infrastructure including a semiconductor plant and oil facilities, though Russian officials emphasized successful interceptions and limited damage, while stray drones entering NATO airspace in Latvia and Lithuania triggered air alerts and political fallout in the Baltic states.

item.title: Trump Warns Iran 'There Won't Be Anything Left' as Drone Strikes Hit Gulf Nuclear and Energy Sites
item.description: Donald Trump issued an ultimatum to Iran warning that 'there won't be anything left of them' if Tehran does not quickly agree to a peace deal, while drone strikes near the UAE's Barakah nuclear plant and in Saudi airspace marked a kinetic escalation in the conflict. Iran responded by vowing to break the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and warning that the Sea of Oman would become America's 'cemetery,' as global energy markets continued to absorb the impact of disrupted shipping lanes. Diplomatic efforts by Pakistan, Russia, France, South Korea, and China continued alongside the military brinkmanship, with Trump scheduled to meet his national security team to discuss next steps.
